speaker: KENT
text: If but as well I other accents borrow

speaker: KENT
text: That can my speech diffuse, my good intent

speaker: KENT
text: May carry through itself to that full issue

speaker: KENT
text: For which I razed my likeness. Now, banished Kent,

speaker: KENT
text: If thou canst serve where thou dost stand condemned,

speaker: KENT
text: So may it come thy master, whom thou lov’st,

speaker: KENT
text: Shall find thee full of labors.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let me not stay a jot for dinner. Go get it ready.

speaker: LEAR
text: How now, what art thou?

speaker: KENT
text: A man, sir.

speaker: LEAR
text: What dost thou profess? What wouldst thou with

speaker: LEAR
text: us?

speaker: KENT
text: I do profess to be no less than I seem, to serve

speaker: KENT
text: him truly that will put me in trust, to love him that

speaker: KENT
text: is honest, to converse with him that is wise and says

speaker: KENT
text: little, to fear judgment, to fight when I cannot

speaker: KENT
text: choose, and to eat no fish.

speaker: LEAR
text: What art thou?

speaker: KENT
text: A very honest-hearted fellow, and as poor as the

speaker: KENT
text: King.

speaker: LEAR
text: If thou be’st as poor for a subject as he’s for a

speaker: LEAR
text: king, thou art poor enough. What wouldst thou?

speaker: KENT
text: Service.

speaker: LEAR
text: Who wouldst thou serve?

speaker: KENT
text: You.

speaker: LEAR
text: Dost thou know me, fellow?

speaker: KENT
text: No, sir, but you have that in your countenance

speaker: KENT
text: which I would fain call master.

speaker: LEAR
text: What’s that?

speaker: KENT
text: Authority.

speaker: LEAR
text: What services canst do?

speaker: KENT
text: I can keep honest counsel, ride, run, mar a

speaker: KENT
text: curious tale in telling it, and deliver a plain message

speaker: KENT
text: bluntly. That which ordinary men are fit for I

speaker: KENT
text: am qualified in, and the best of me is diligence.

speaker: LEAR
text: How old art thou?

speaker: KENT
text: Not so young, sir, to love a woman for singing,

speaker: KENT
text: nor so old to dote on her for anything. I have years

speaker: KENT
text: on my back forty-eight.

speaker: LEAR
text: Follow me. Thou shalt serve me—if I like thee

speaker: LEAR
text: no worse after dinner. I will not part from thee

speaker: LEAR
text: yet.—Dinner, ho, dinner!—Where’s my knave, my

speaker: LEAR
text: Fool? Go you and call my Fool hither.

speaker: LEAR
text: You, you, sirrah, where’s my daughter?

speaker: OSWALD
text: So please you—

speaker: LEAR
text: What says the fellow there? Call the clotpole

speaker: LEAR
text:
back.
                 
                A
                 
                Knight
                 
                exits
                .
               Where’s my Fool? Ho! I think

speaker: LEAR
text: the world’s asleep.

speaker: LEAR
text: How now? Where’s that mongrel?

speaker: KNIGHT
text: He says, my lord, your daughter is not well.

speaker: LEAR
text: Why came not the slave back to me when I

speaker: LEAR
text: called him?

speaker: KNIGHT
text: Sir, he answered me in the roundest manner,

speaker: KNIGHT
text: he would not.

speaker: LEAR
text: He would not?

speaker: KNIGHT
text: My lord, I know not what the matter is, but to

speaker: KNIGHT
text: my judgment your Highness is not entertained

speaker: KNIGHT
text: with that ceremonious affection as you were wont.

speaker: KNIGHT
text: There’s a great abatement of kindness appears as

speaker: KNIGHT
text: well in the general dependents as in the Duke

speaker: KNIGHT
text: himself also, and your daughter.

speaker: LEAR
text: Ha? Sayst thou so?

speaker: KNIGHT
text: I beseech you pardon me, my lord, if I be

speaker: KNIGHT
text: mistaken, for my duty cannot be silent when I think

speaker: KNIGHT
text: your Highness wronged.

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou but remembrest me of mine own conception.

speaker: LEAR
text: I have perceived a most faint neglect of late,

speaker: LEAR
text: which I have rather blamed as mine own jealous

speaker: LEAR
text: curiosity than as a very pretense and purpose of

speaker: LEAR
text: unkindness. I will look further into ’t. But where’s

speaker: LEAR
text: my Fool? I have not seen him this two days.

speaker: KNIGHT
text: Since my young lady’s going into France, sir,

speaker: KNIGHT
text: the Fool hath much pined away.

speaker: LEAR
text: No more of that. I have noted it well.—Go you

speaker: LEAR
text: and tell my daughter I would speak with her.

speaker: LEAR
text: Go you call hither my Fool.

speaker: LEAR
text: O you, sir, you, come you hither, sir. Who am I, sir?

speaker: OSWALD
text: My lady’s father.

speaker: LEAR
text: “My lady’s father”? My lord’s knave! You whoreson

speaker: LEAR
text: dog, you slave, you cur!

speaker: OSWALD
text: I am none of these, my lord, I beseech your

speaker: OSWALD
text: pardon.

speaker: LEAR
text: Do you bandy looks with me, you rascal?

speaker: OSWALD
text: I’ll not be strucken, my lord.

speaker: KENT
text: Nor tripped neither, you base

speaker: KENT
text: football player?

speaker: LEAR
text: I thank thee, fellow. Thou serv’st me, and I’ll

speaker: LEAR
text: love thee.

speaker: KENT
text: Come, sir, arise. Away. I’ll teach you

speaker: KENT
text: differences. Away, away. If you will measure your

speaker: KENT
text: lubber’s length again, tarry. But away. Go to. Have

speaker: KENT
text: you wisdom? So.

speaker: LEAR
text: Now, my friendly knave, I thank thee. There’s

speaker: LEAR
text: earnest of thy service.

speaker: FOOL
text:
Let me hire him too.
                 
                To
                 
                Kent
                .
               Here’s my

speaker: FOOL
text: coxcomb.

speaker: LEAR
text: How now, my pretty knave, how dost thou?

speaker: FOOL
text: Sirrah, you were best take my

speaker: FOOL
text: coxcomb.

speaker: LEAR
text: Why, my boy?

speaker: FOOL
text: Why? For taking one’s part that’s out of favor.

speaker: FOOL
text:
To
                 
                Kent
                .
               Nay, an thou canst not smile as the

speaker: FOOL
text: wind sits, thou ’lt catch cold shortly. There, take my

speaker: FOOL
text: coxcomb. Why, this fellow has banished two on ’s

speaker: FOOL
text: daughters and did the third a blessing against his

speaker: FOOL
text: will. If thou follow him, thou must needs wear my

speaker: FOOL
text: coxcomb.—How now, nuncle? Would I had two

speaker: FOOL
text: coxcombs and two daughters.

speaker: LEAR
text: Why, my boy?

speaker: FOOL
text: If I gave them all my living, I’d keep my coxcombs

speaker: FOOL
text: myself. There’s mine. Beg another of thy

speaker: FOOL
text: daughters.

speaker: LEAR
text: Take heed, sirrah—the whip.

speaker: FOOL
text: Truth’s a dog must to kennel; he must be

speaker: FOOL
text: whipped out, when the Lady Brach may stand by th’

speaker: FOOL
text: fire and stink.

speaker: LEAR
text: A pestilent gall to me!

speaker: FOOL
text: Sirrah, I’ll teach thee a speech.

speaker: LEAR
text: Do.

speaker: FOOL
text: Mark it, nuncle:

speaker: FOOL
text: Have more than thou showest.

speaker: FOOL
text: Speak less than thou knowest,

speaker: FOOL
text: Lend less than thou owest,

speaker: FOOL
text: Ride more than thou goest,

speaker: FOOL
text: Learn more than thou trowest,

speaker: FOOL
text: Set less than thou throwest;

speaker: FOOL
text: Leave thy drink and thy whore

speaker: FOOL
text: And keep in-a-door,

speaker: FOOL
text: And thou shalt have more

speaker: FOOL
text: Than two tens to a score.

speaker: KENT
text: This is nothing, Fool.

speaker: FOOL
text: Then ’tis like the breath of an unfee’d lawyer.

speaker: FOOL
text: You gave me nothing for ’t.—Can you make no use

speaker: FOOL
text: of nothing, nuncle?

speaker: LEAR
text: Why no, boy. Nothing can be made out of

speaker: LEAR
text: nothing.

speaker: FOOL
text: Prithee tell him, so much the rent of his

speaker: FOOL
text: land comes to. He will not believe a Fool.

speaker: LEAR
text: A bitter Fool!

speaker: FOOL
text: Dost know the difference, my boy, between a

speaker: FOOL
text: bitter fool and a sweet one?

speaker: LEAR
text: No, lad, teach me.

speaker: FOOL
text: That lord that counseled thee

speaker: FOOL
text: To give away thy land,

speaker: FOOL
text: Come place him here by me;

speaker: FOOL
text: Do thou for him stand.

speaker: FOOL
text: The sweet and bitter fool

speaker: FOOL
text: Will presently appear:

speaker: FOOL
text: The one in motley here,

speaker: FOOL
text: The other found out there.

speaker: LEAR
text: Dost thou call me “fool,” boy?

speaker: FOOL
text: All thy other titles thou hast given away. That

speaker: FOOL
text: thou wast born with.

speaker: KENT
text: This is not altogether fool, my lord.

speaker: FOOL
text: No, faith, lords and great men will not let me. If

speaker: FOOL
text: I had a monopoly out, they would have part on ’t.

speaker: FOOL
text: And ladies too, they will not let me have all the fool

speaker: FOOL
text: to myself; they’ll be snatching.—Nuncle, give me

speaker: FOOL
text: an egg, and I’ll give thee two crowns.

speaker: LEAR
text: What two crowns shall they be?

speaker: FOOL
text: Why, after I have cut the egg i’ th’ middle and eat

speaker: FOOL
text: up the meat, the two crowns of the egg. When thou

speaker: FOOL
text: clovest thy crown i’ th’ middle and gav’st away

speaker: FOOL
text: both parts, thou bor’st thine ass on thy back o’er

speaker: FOOL
text: the dirt. Thou hadst little wit in thy bald crown

speaker: FOOL
text: when thou gav’st thy golden one away. If I speak

speaker: FOOL
text: like myself in this, let him be whipped that first

speaker: FOOL
text: finds it so.

speaker: FOOL
text: Fools had ne’er less grace in a year,

speaker: FOOL
text: For wise men are grown foppish

speaker: FOOL
text: And know not how their wits to wear,

speaker: FOOL
text: Their manners are so apish.

speaker: LEAR
text: When were you wont to be so full of songs,

speaker: LEAR
text: sirrah?

speaker: FOOL
text: I have used it, nuncle, e’er since thou mad’st thy

speaker: FOOL
text: daughters thy mothers. For when thou gav’st them

speaker: FOOL
text: the rod and put’st down thine own breeches,

speaker: FOOL
text: Then they for sudden joy did weep,

speaker: FOOL
text: And I for sorrow sung,

speaker: FOOL
text: That such a king should play bo-peep

speaker: FOOL
text: And go the fools among.

speaker: FOOL
text: Prithee, nuncle, keep a schoolmaster that can teach

speaker: FOOL
text: thy Fool to lie. I would fain learn to lie.

speaker: LEAR
text: An you lie, sirrah, we’ll have you whipped.

speaker: FOOL
text: I marvel what kin thou and thy daughters are.

speaker: FOOL
text: They’ll have me whipped for speaking true, thou ’lt

speaker: FOOL
text: have me whipped for lying, and sometimes I am

speaker: FOOL
text: whipped for holding my peace. I had rather be any

speaker: FOOL
text: kind o’ thing than a Fool. And yet I would not be

speaker: FOOL
text: thee, nuncle. Thou hast pared thy wit o’ both sides

speaker: FOOL
text: and left nothing i’ th’ middle. Here comes one o’ the

speaker: FOOL
text: parings.

speaker: LEAR
text: How now, daughter? What makes that frontlet on?

speaker: LEAR
text: Methinks you are too much of late i’ th’ frown.

speaker: FOOL
text: Thou wast a pretty fellow when thou hadst no

speaker: FOOL
text: need to care for her frowning. Now thou art an O

speaker: FOOL
text: without a figure. I am better than thou art now. I

speaker: FOOL
text:
am a Fool. Thou art nothing.
                 
                To
                 
                Goneril
                .
               Yes,

speaker: FOOL
text: forsooth, I will hold my tongue. So your face bids

speaker: FOOL
text: me, though you say nothing.

speaker: FOOL
text: Mum, mum,

speaker: FOOL
text: He that keeps nor crust nor crumb,

speaker: FOOL
text: Weary of all, shall want some.

speaker: FOOL
text: That’s a shelled peascod.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Not only, sir, this your all-licensed Fool,

speaker: GONERIL
text: But other of your insolent retinue

speaker: GONERIL
text: Do hourly carp and quarrel, breaking forth

speaker: GONERIL
text: In rank and not-to-be-endurèd riots. Sir,

speaker: GONERIL
text: I had thought by making this well known unto you

speaker: GONERIL
text: To have found a safe redress, but now grow fearful,

speaker: GONERIL
text: By what yourself too late have spoke and done,

speaker: GONERIL
text: That you protect this course and put it on

speaker: GONERIL
text: By your allowance; which if you should, the fault

speaker: GONERIL
text: Would not ’scape censure, nor the redresses sleep

speaker: GONERIL
text: Which in the tender of a wholesome weal

speaker: GONERIL
text: Might in their working do you that offense,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Which else were shame, that then necessity

speaker: GONERIL
text: Will call discreet proceeding.

speaker: FOOL
text: For you know, nuncle,

speaker: FOOL
text: The hedge-sparrow fed the cuckoo so long,

speaker: FOOL
text: That it’s had it head bit off by it young.

speaker: FOOL
text: So out went the candle, and we were left darkling.

speaker: LEAR
text: Are you our daughter?

speaker: GONERIL
text: I would you would make use of your good wisdom,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Whereof I know you are fraught, and put away

speaker: GONERIL
text: These dispositions which of late transport you

speaker: GONERIL
text: From what you rightly are.

speaker: FOOL
text: May not an ass know when the cart draws the

speaker: FOOL
text: horse? Whoop, Jug, I love thee!

speaker: LEAR
text: Does any here know me? This is not Lear.

speaker: LEAR
text: Does Lear walk thus, speak thus? Where are his eyes?

speaker: LEAR
text: Either his notion weakens, his discernings

speaker: LEAR
text: Are lethargied—Ha! Waking? ’Tis not so.

speaker: LEAR
text: Who is it that can tell me who I am?

speaker: FOOL
text: Lear’s shadow.

speaker: LEAR
text: I would learn that, for, by the marks of sovereignty,

speaker: LEAR
text: Knowledge, and reason, I should be false persuaded

speaker: LEAR
text: I had daughters.

speaker: FOOL
text: Which they will make an obedient father.

speaker: LEAR
text: Your name, fair gentlewoman?

speaker: GONERIL
text: This admiration, sir, is much o’ th’ savor

speaker: GONERIL
text: Of other your new pranks. I do beseech you

speaker: GONERIL
text: To understand my purposes aright.

speaker: GONERIL
text: As you are old and reverend, should be wise.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Here do you keep a hundred knights and squires,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Men so disordered, so debauched and bold,

speaker: GONERIL
text: That this our court, infected with their manners,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Shows like a riotous inn. Epicurism and lust

speaker: GONERIL
text: Makes it more like a tavern or a brothel

speaker: GONERIL
text: Than a graced palace. The shame itself doth speak

speaker: GONERIL
text: For instant remedy. Be then desired,

speaker: GONERIL
text: By her that else will take the thing she begs,

speaker: GONERIL
text: A little to disquantity your train,

speaker: GONERIL
text: And the remainders that shall still depend

speaker: GONERIL
text: To be such men as may besort your age,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Which know themselves and you.

speaker: LEAR
text: Darkness and devils!—

speaker: LEAR
text: Saddle my horses. Call my train together.

speaker: LEAR
text: Degenerate bastard, I’ll not trouble thee.

speaker: LEAR
text: Yet have I left a daughter.

speaker: GONERIL
text: You strike my people, and your disordered rabble

speaker: GONERIL
text: Make servants of their betters.

speaker: LEAR
text: Woe that too late repents!—O, sir, are you come?

speaker: LEAR
text: Is it your will? Speak, sir.—Prepare my horses.

speaker: LEAR
text: Ingratitude, thou marble-hearted fiend,

speaker: LEAR
text: More hideous when thou show’st thee in a child

speaker: LEAR
text: Than the sea monster!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Pray, sir, be patient.

speaker: LEAR
text: Detested kite, thou liest.

speaker: LEAR
text: My train are men of choice and rarest parts,

speaker: LEAR
text: That all particulars of duty know

speaker: LEAR
text: And in the most exact regard support

speaker: LEAR
text: The worships of their name. O most small fault,

speaker: LEAR
text: How ugly didst thou in Cordelia show,

speaker: LEAR
text: Which, like an engine, wrenched my frame of nature

speaker: LEAR
text: From the fixed place, drew from my heart all love

speaker: LEAR
text: And added to the gall! O Lear, Lear, Lear!

speaker: LEAR
text: Beat at this gate that let thy folly in

speaker: LEAR
text: And thy dear judgment out. Go, go, my people.

speaker: ALBANY
text: My lord, I am guiltless as I am ignorant

speaker: ALBANY
text: Of what hath moved you.

speaker: LEAR
text: It may be so, my lord.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Hear, Nature, hear, dear goddess, hear!

speaker: LEAR
text: Suspend thy purpose if thou didst intend

speaker: LEAR
text: To make this creature fruitful.

speaker: LEAR
text: Into her womb convey sterility.

speaker: LEAR
text: Dry up in her the organs of increase,

speaker: LEAR
text: And from her derogate body never spring

speaker: LEAR
text: A babe to honor her. If she must teem,

speaker: LEAR
text: Create her child of spleen, that it may live

speaker: LEAR
text: And be a thwart disnatured torment to her.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let it stamp wrinkles in her brow of youth,

speaker: LEAR
text: With cadent tears fret channels in her cheeks,

speaker: LEAR
text: Turn all her mother’s pains and benefits

speaker: LEAR
text: To laughter and contempt, that she may feel

speaker: LEAR
text: How sharper than a serpent’s tooth it is

speaker: LEAR
text: To have a thankless child.—Away, away!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Now, gods that we adore, whereof comes this?

speaker: GONERIL
text: Never afflict yourself to know more of it,

speaker: GONERIL
text: But let his disposition have that scope

speaker: GONERIL
text: As dotage gives it.

speaker: LEAR
text: What, fifty of my followers at a clap?

speaker: LEAR
text: Within a fortnight?

speaker: ALBANY
text: What’s the matter, sir?

speaker: LEAR
text:
I’ll tell thee.
                 
                To
                 
                Goneril
                .
               Life and death! I am ashamed

speaker: LEAR
text: That thou hast power to shake my manhood thus,

speaker: LEAR
text: That these hot tears, which break from me perforce,

speaker: LEAR
text: Should make thee worth them. Blasts and fogs upon thee!

speaker: LEAR
text: Th’ untented woundings of a father’s curse

speaker: LEAR
text: Pierce every sense about thee! Old fond eyes,

speaker: LEAR
text: Beweep this cause again, I’ll pluck you out

speaker: LEAR
text: And cast you, with the waters that you loose,

speaker: LEAR
text: To temper clay. Yea, is ’t come to this?

speaker: LEAR
text: Ha! Let it be so. I have another daughter

speaker: LEAR
text: Who, I am sure, is kind and comfortable.

speaker: LEAR
text: When she shall hear this of thee, with her nails

speaker: LEAR
text: She’ll flay thy wolvish visage. Thou shalt find

speaker: LEAR
text: That I’ll resume the shape which thou dost think

speaker: LEAR
text: I have cast off forever.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Do you mark that?

speaker: ALBANY
text: I cannot be so partial, Goneril,

speaker: ALBANY
text: To the great love I bear you—

speaker: GONERIL
text: Pray you, content.—What, Oswald, ho!—

speaker: GONERIL
text: You, sir, more knave than Fool, after your master.

speaker: FOOL
text: Nuncle Lear, Nuncle Lear, tarry. Take the Fool

speaker: FOOL
text: with thee.

speaker: FOOL
text: A fox, when one has caught her,

speaker: FOOL
text: And such a daughter,

speaker: FOOL
text: Should sure to the slaughter,

speaker: FOOL
text: If my cap would buy a halter.

speaker: FOOL
text: So the Fool follows after.

speaker: GONERIL
text: This man hath had good counsel. A hundred knights!

speaker: GONERIL
text: ’Tis politic and safe to let him keep

speaker: GONERIL
text: At point a hundred knights! Yes, that on every dream,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Each buzz, each fancy, each complaint, dislike,

speaker: GONERIL
text: He may enguard his dotage with their powers

speaker: GONERIL
text: And hold our lives in mercy.—Oswald, I say!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Well, you may fear too far.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Safer than trust too far.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Let me still take away the harms I fear,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Not fear still to be taken. I know his heart.

speaker: GONERIL
text: What he hath uttered I have writ my sister.

speaker: GONERIL
text: If she sustain him and his hundred knights

speaker: GONERIL
text: When I have showed th’ unfitness—

speaker: GONERIL
text: How now, Oswald?

speaker: GONERIL
text: What, have you writ that letter to my sister?

speaker: OSWALD
text: Ay, madam.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Take you some company and away to horse.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Inform her full of my particular fear,

speaker: GONERIL
text: And thereto add such reasons of your own

speaker: GONERIL
text: As may compact it more. Get you gone,

speaker: GONERIL
text:
And hasten your return.
                 
                Oswald
                 
                exits
                .
               No, no, my lord,

speaker: GONERIL
text: This milky gentleness and course of yours,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Though I condemn not, yet, under pardon,

speaker: GONERIL
text: You are much more at task for want of wisdom

speaker: GONERIL
text: Than praised for harmful mildness.

speaker: ALBANY
text: How far your eyes may pierce I cannot tell.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Striving to better, oft we mar what’s well.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Nay, then—

speaker: ALBANY
text: Well, well, th’ event.
